system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors, both stemming from the `blockMeshDict`:

### Error 1: Block Inside-Out (hex 8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)

**Error Message:**
```
Block hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11) ... is inside-out
```

This is the **third attempt** fixing the blockMeshDict. Looking at the current block definition:

```
hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```

Vertices:
- 8: (25, 0, 25)
- 12: (-500, 0, 25)
- 13: (-500, 0, -25)
- 9: (25, 0, -25)
- 10: (25, 25, 25)
- 14: (-500, 25, 25)
- 15: (-500, 25, -25)
- 11: (25, 25, -25)

The problem is that vertices 8 and 9 are at x=25 while vertices 10 and 11 are also at x=25. But vertices 12, 13, 14, 15 are at x=-500. This creates a block where the "top" face (8,9,10,11) and "bottom" face (12,13,14,15) don't form valid parallel hexahedral faces — the winding order is inconsistent across the block.

More critically, looking at the **entire mesh structure**: the bend region uses vertices 8,9,10,11 at x=25, but these are positioned such that when connected to the outlet vertices at x=-500, the resulting hexahedron has incorrect vertex ordering/winding.

### Root Cause Across All Attempts

The fundamental issue is that the **vertex positions and block connectivity don't form valid hexahedral blocks**. Specifically, the bend transition block `hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)` connects vertices from three different x-planes in a way that violates the right-hand rule for hexahedral winding.

### Proposed Solution — Different Approach Than Previous Attempts

Instead of trying to force a 3-block structure with problematic vertex placements, **restructure the mesh to use only 2 blocks** with properly ordered vertices:

#### Option: Simplify to 2 Blocks (Split Only at the Bend Plane)

Keep vertices 0-7 at x=-50 (inlet face) and vertices 4-11 at x=0 (mid-plane), but **remove the outlet-level vertices entirely** and let the outlet boundary be formed by the far ends of the existing blocks. Or more practically:

**Redefine the mesh with just 2 blocks:**

1. **Block 1:** `hex (0 4 5 1 2 6 7 3)` — the inlet-to-mid-section block (unchanged from Attempt 1, should be valid now)
2. **Block 2:** `hex (4 8 9 5 6 10 11 7)` — the bend/mid-to-outlet block, but reordered correctly

For Block 2, the vertex ordering needs to follow the right-hand rule. Try:
```
hex (4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
```
Where:
- Face 1: (4,5,9,8) — bottom face at y=0, z from +25 to -25
- Face 2: (6,7,11,10) — top face at y=25, z from +25 to -25
- Side faces connect appropriately

If that still causes issues, **use a different split strategy**: place all four outlet vertices at their correct x=-500 positions but define only **one** block connecting the inlet face (0,1,2,3) to the outlet face (12,13,14,15), and use edge splits to create intermediate levels. However, the simplest robust fix is:

**Use a single block with local refinements via edges** — remove the middle block entirely and define a single hex block `hex (0 4 5 1 2 6 7 3)` covering the full length, with edge subdivisions creating the necessary face counts. Then define the outlet boundary using the far faces of this single block.

#### Key Fixes Needed:

1. **Remove block 3** (`hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11)`) completely — it's fundamentally problematic.
2. **Replace with either**:
   - A single block with edge subdivisions, OR
   - Two blocks with correct winding orders (e.g., `hex (4 5 9 8 6 7 11 10)` for the second block)
3. **Update all face definitions** to match the new vertex coordinates and block count.
4. Ensure the **outlet boundary** is properly defined using the far faces of whatever blocks span the full domain length.

The critical principle: **every hex block must have vertices ordered so that the bottom face (first 4 vertices) and top face (last 4 vertices) follow the right-hand rule, and corresponding side faces are consistently oriented.**</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1500. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>
Return strict JSON now with key target_files only.
response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace all 3 blocks with a single hex block: hex (0 4 5 1 2 6 7 3) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) -- this is the inlet-to-mid-plane block unchanged; Add a second hex block: hex (4 8 9 5 6 10 11 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) -- this connects mid-plane to outlet; Redefine outlet boundary faces as (12 13 15 14) from the third block; Redefine upperWall faces as (0 4 6 2) and (4 8 10 6); Redefine lowerWall faces as (1 5 7 3) and (5 9 11 7); Keep frontAndBack faces as (0 2 6 4) and (4 6 10 8) and add (8 10 14 12) for the outlet-facing face of the third logical segment; Actually rewrite entire block section to have 3 proper hex blocks: first block hex (0 4 5 1 2 6 7 3) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1), second block hex (4 8 9 5 6 10 11 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1), third block hex (8 12 13 9 10 14 15 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) but fix the vertex ordering to be consistent: hex (8 9 11 10 12 13 15 14) would be better but we need to keep the pattern; Replace the third block definition with hex (8 9 11 10 12 13 15 14) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) which has bottom face 8 9 11 10 and top face 12 13 15 14 -- this follows right-hand rule where if you curl fingers from 8->9->11->10 your thumb points in the block normal direction')]
